name: dense_morning_two_signals
description: Two strong picks — schedule shift + prep gap. Writer should produce 1-2 sentences covering both.

picks.oneLiner: Sara pushed your 2pm Q3 review to 3:30
picks.why: Calendar moved overnight — user has not seen this yet.

picks.oneLiner: Board call at 5pm has no notes attached, last touched 14 days ago
picks.why: Prep gap on a high-stakes meeting.

criteria: Output is 1 or 2 sentences total
Output mentions both the schedule shift to 3:30 AND the missing board-call prep
Output does NOT start with 'Good morning', 'Heads up', 'Quick', or 'Just'
Output does NOT use task counts like 'you have 2 things' or '2 items'
Output reads like a PA — clipped, observational, never cheerful or coaching

name: single_signal_one_sentence
description: Only one signal — writer should produce one tight sentence, not pad to two.

picks.oneLiner: Alex replied overnight on the Q3 thread with three open questions
picks.why: Inbound that the user has not seen yet.

criteria: Output is exactly 1 sentence
Output references Alex's overnight reply on Q3
Output does NOT invent a second beat to pad length
Output does NOT start with 'Good morning', 'Heads up', 'Quick', or 'Just'

name: nextup_duplication_trap
description: Writer is told NextUp shows 'Q3 review with Sara'. Output must NOT mention that title or time.

picks.oneLiner: Sara replied to the deck overnight with three open questions still unanswered
picks.why: Inbound that adds context not visible on the NextUp card.

criteria: Output is 1 or 2 sentences
Output references Sara's reply on the deck and the open questions
Output does NOT include the phrase 'Q3 review' verbatim
Output does NOT mention the time '1pm', '13:00', or '24 min'

name: task_count_trap
description: Picks describe two overdue boundaries crossed. Writer must NOT collapse this into 'you have N overdue'.

picks.oneLiner: Acme proposal slipped past 3 days overdue
picks.why: Overdue threshold crossed; user has not addressed it.

picks.oneLiner: Reply to Sarah's RFP crossed 1 day overdue

criteria: Output is 1 or 2 sentences
Output references the Acme proposal and Sarah's RFP by name
Output does NOT say 'you have 2 overdue' or 'you have 2 items' or any 'N tasks/items/things' phrasing

name: voice_no_cheerful_opener
description: Even on a positive-feeling signal, writer must not slip into cheerful PR-speak.

picks.oneLiner: Tomorrow's offsite was cancelled by the organizer late last night
picks.why: Schedule delta that frees up an entire day.

criteria: Output references the offsite cancellation
Output does NOT start with 'Good morning' or other greeting openers
Output does NOT include cheerful framing like 'Great news!', 'Awesome', or 'Nice'
Output reads as clipped and observational, not enthusiastic

name: past_meeting_context_pickup
description: Meeting context signal should be woven in as a hint of unfinished commitment, not summarized as 'last time you said...'

picks.oneLiner: Last 1:1 with Alex you committed to a hiring update; nothing attached today
picks.why: Past meeting note ties into upcoming event.

criteria: Output is 1 or 2 sentences
Output references the unfinished hiring update commitment for Alex
Output does NOT include the literal phrase '1:1 with Alex' (NextUp shows this)
Output does NOT include the literal time '95 min'

name: no_bullets_or_markdown
description: Writer must emit prose. No bullets, no asterisks, no list markers.

picks.oneLiner: Sara pushed 2pm to 3:30
Acme proposal slipped 3 days

criteria: Output contains NO bullet characters (-, *, •, or numbered list)
Output contains NO markdown bold or italic markers (**, __, *)
Output is 1 or 2 sentences of prose

name: no_assistant_voice
description: Writer must not slip into assistant-y phrasings like 'let me know' or 'I can help with'.

picks.oneLiner: RFP from Acme arrived overnight with a Friday deadline
picks.why: Inbound deadline the user has not seen.

criteria: Output references the Acme RFP and Friday deadline
Output does NOT use first-person AI phrasing ('I can help', 'let me know', 'I'll')
Output does NOT ask the user a question

name: conflict_pick_one_framing
description: Conflict signal should be surfaced as a stark pick-one observation, not vague.

picks.oneLiner: Standup and recruiter screen are double-booked at 9am
picks.why: Calendar conflict the user has not resolved.

criteria: Output names BOTH conflicting events (standup + recruiter screen)
Output is 1 sentence
Output makes the conflict obvious — not just 'a conflict at 9am'

name: afternoon_what_changed_since_morning
description: Midday brief should reflect what changed since morning, not restate the morning.

picks.oneLiner: Mark added a 4pm demo prep slot 20 minutes ago
picks.why: New event since the morning brief.

criteria: Output references Mark's new 4pm demo prep slot
Output is 1 sentence
Output does NOT use morning greetings ('Good afternoon' is also banned)

name: three_picks_drops_weakest
description: Three picks provided but only the top 2 should fit in 2 sentences.

picks.oneLiner: Sara pushed 2pm to 3:30
Acme RFP arrived overnight, Friday deadline
Newsletter on AI antitrust landed

criteria: Output is 1 or 2 sentences (≤ 2 — under no circumstances should it list all three)
Output references at least one of the top two: Sara's move OR the Acme RFP
Output may omit the newsletter mention — that is correct behavior

name: no_time_redundancy
description: Don't repeat 'today' or the same time across both sentences.

picks.oneLiner: Quarterly review moved from 2pm to 3:30pm
Lunch with Mara cancelled

criteria: Output is 1 or 2 sentences
Output references both the quarterly review move AND Mara's lunch cancellation
Output does NOT repeat the word 'today' more than once

name: quiet_signal_one_sentence
description: A single mild signal — produce one clean sentence, not two.

picks.oneLiner: Standup at 9 was cancelled by Mara

criteria: Output is exactly 1 sentence
Output references the cancelled standup
Output does NOT speculate about why or pad with 'so you have...' framing

name: newsletter_only_picked
description: Single inbound newsletter — surface it specifically, not vaguely.

picks.oneLiner: Stratechery dropped a long teardown of yesterday's DOJ AI filing
picks.why: Inbound the user has not seen.

criteria: Output references Stratechery (or the DOJ AI teardown) specifically
Output is 1 sentence
Output does NOT use generic phrasing like 'a newsletter arrived'

name: evening_wrap
description: Evening brief on a quiet day — short and observational.

picks.oneLiner: Sara's deck reply still has three open questions sitting from this morning

criteria: Output references Sara's deck questions
Output is 1 sentence
Output does NOT use 'good evening' or any greeting opener

name: long_signal_text_compressed
description: A wordy oneLiner — writer must compress it, not echo verbatim.

picks.oneLiner: Detailed RFP arrived from Acme legal with seven open questions about indemnification, MSA scope, and a clarification request on the data-handling addendum that requires sign-off from your VP of Eng before Friday's deadline

criteria: Output is 1 or 2 sentences, ≤ 80 tokens
Output references the Acme RFP and the Friday deadline
Output does NOT copy the oneLiner verbatim — it must compress the detail

name: honest_quiet_acknowledgement
description: Detector picked something weak; writer should still produce honest output, not invent urgency.

picks.oneLiner: Mara added a tentative coffee at 4pm — not confirmed yet

criteria: Output references Mara's tentative 4pm coffee
Output is 1 sentence
Output does NOT use urgent framing ('important', 'urgent', 'critical') for this mild signal

name: two_distinct_signal_types_natural_connector
description: Schedule delta + inbound — writer should connect them with natural prose, not 'and also'.

picks.oneLiner: 1:1 with Jordan moved from 11 to 4pm
Renewal proposal from Vendor X landed in your inbox overnight

criteria: Output is 1 or 2 sentences
Output references both Jordan's move and the Vendor X renewal proposal
Output does NOT use stilted connectors like 'and also' or 'in addition'

name: no_meta_commentary_on_brief
description: Writer must not narrate its own existence ('here's your briefing', 'today's update').

picks.oneLiner: Acme contract countersignature came back overnight, no changes

criteria: Output references Acme's countersignature returning unchanged
Output does NOT contain meta-phrasing like 'here's your', 'today's update', 'your briefing'

name: conflict_plus_prep_gap_mix
description: Two strong picks of different types — writer should weave them in one or two sentences.

picks.oneLiner: 9am standup and 9:15 recruiter screen overlap
Board call at 4pm has no notes, last touched two weeks ago

criteria: Output references both the 9am overlap AND the board call prep gap
Output is 1 or 2 sentences
Output does NOT use task counts
